message: bd-4uyu5 implement FUSE write intent handling

 {"id":"bd-4ogh","title":"Implement ext4/btrfs parity features: decompression, inline data, bigalloc, ZERO_RANGE","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-03-22T18:28:47.537528441Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.634096851Z","closed_at":"2026-03-22T18:29:02.633525312Z","close_reason":"Implemented 4 new parity features:
(1) btrfs transparent decompression: ZLIB (flate2) + ZSTD in read path for inline and regular extents. Added ram_bytes field to BtrfsExtentData enum. LZO deferred (custom framing).
(2) ext4 inline data read: reads from inode extent_bytes + xattr_ibody when EXT4_INLINE_DATA_FL is set. Removed INLINE_DATA from REJECTED_V1 incompat set.
(3) ext4 bigalloc infrastructure: added cluster_ratio to FsGeometry (cluster_size/block_size for BIGALLOC, else 1).
(4) ext4 ZERO_RANGE fallocate: writes zero blocks to existing allocated extents in range. Mode 0x10 now accepted.
All tests pass (471 ffs-core, 360 ffs-ondisk, 142 ffs-alloc, 117 ffs-btrfs, 114 ffs-extent).","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","ext4","parity"]}
 {"id":"bd-4q7p","title":"Fix mark_written count=0 edge case: creates zero-length extent and corrupts tree","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-26T01:35:19.522825993Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-26T01:41:13.588091160Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T01:41:13.588068958Z","close_reason":"Added early return for count=0 in mark_written and punch_hole. Added tests confirming noop behavior.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4qb1o","title":"port fscrypt policy-ex ioctl parity in ffs-fuse and ffs-core","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-04-21T01:24:01.023080784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:23.330710277Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:14:23.327706536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-4uyu5","title":"[FUSE] RWF_DSYNC + io_uring per-op flags propagation","description":"Honor pwritev2(2) RWF_* flags (RWF_SYNC, RWF_DSYNC, RWF_HIPRI, RWF_NOWAIT, RWF_APPEND) and io_uring per-SQE flags (IOSQE_FIXED_FILE, IOSQE_IO_LINK, IOSQE_ASYNC) when the kernel forwards them through the FUSE WRITE / IORING_OP_WRITE path.

Context:
- Linux pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC must commit the write durably (data + minimal metadata) before returning. ext4 implements this via filemap_write_and_wait_range + ext4_sync_file. FrankenFS should map RWF_DSYNC to: stage write -> jbd2 commit_transaction -> dev.sync (data only, not full inode metadata journal).
- RWF_NOWAIT must return EAGAIN rather than block on a contended lock (matches ext4's mapping_check_writeback flag); for ffs-fuse, this means try_lock on FuseInodeLocks instead of blocking acquire.
- io_uring forwards fuse_in_header.flags including FUSE_IOCB_NOWAIT (bit 9) and FUSE_IOCB_DSYNC; the dispatcher needs to read these and route accordingly.

Acceptance:
- A test that issues pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC, then drops the underlying device dirty cache, then re-mounts -> data is recoverable (no FUA but still durable per the journal commit).
- pwritev2 with RWF_NOWAIT on a parent inode currently held by another mutation returns EAGAIN within 100us, not a multi-ms block.
- xfstests generic/{623,633,634} (RWF_*) pass.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:56.562180780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T21:37:54.952637798Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"bd-4uyu5","title":"[FUSE] RWF_DSYNC + io_uring per-op flags propagation","description":"Honor pwritev2(2) RWF_* flags (RWF_SYNC, RWF_DSYNC, RWF_HIPRI, RWF_NOWAIT, RWF_APPEND) and io_uring per-SQE flags (IOSQE_FIXED_FILE, IOSQE_IO_LINK, IOSQE_ASYNC) when the kernel forwards them through the FUSE WRITE / IORING_OP_WRITE path.

Context:
- Linux pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC must commit the write durably (data + minimal metadata) before returning. ext4 implements this via filemap_write_and_wait_range + ext4_sync_file. FrankenFS should map RWF_DSYNC to: stage write -> jbd2 commit_transaction -> dev.sync (data only, not full inode metadata journal).
- RWF_NOWAIT must return EAGAIN rather than block on a contended lock (matches ext4's mapping_check_writeback flag); for ffs-fuse, this means try_lock on FuseInodeLocks instead of blocking acquire.
- io_uring forwards fuse_in_header.flags including FUSE_IOCB_NOWAIT (bit 9) and FUSE_IOCB_DSYNC; the dispatcher needs to read these and route accordingly.

Acceptance:
- A test that issues pwritev2 with RWF_DSYNC, then drops the underlying device dirty cache, then re-mounts -> data is recoverable (no FUA but still durable per the journal commit).
- pwritev2 with RWF_NOWAIT on a parent inode currently held by another mutation returns EAGAIN within 100us, not a multi-ms block.
- xfstests generic/{623,633,634} (RWF_*) pass.","notes":"Implemented fuser-exposed WRITE intent handling: O_DSYNC/O_SYNC flags now trigger data/full fsync after commit; RWF_NOWAIT uses nonblocking inode mutation-lock acquisition and returns EAGAIN; readahead invalidates immediately after commit even if the requested sync fails. Added unit coverage for sync, dsync, write_flags collision, nowait contention, and sync-failure cache invalidation plus a mounted pwritev2 RWF_DSYNC harness probe. Targeted validation passed: cargo test -p ffs-fuse dispatch_write_; cargo test -p ffs-harness --test fuse_e2e ext4_fuse_pwritev2_rwf_dsync_persists_written_data_after_remount (soft-skipped mount on RCH permission); cargo check/clippy for ffs-fuse+ffs-harness; parity_report_matches_feature_parity_md; cargo fmt --check; UBS touched files. Remaining external validation: xfstests generic/{623,633,634} and full mounted FUSE pwritev2 durability on a host that permits FUSE mounts.","status":"blocked","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-25T05:25:56.562180780Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-25T22:07:06.958690858Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-4wbbn","title":"Add golden contract for representative external xattr block encoding","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T00:16:04.399392196Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T00:24:55.596702273Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T00:24:55.594435705Z","close_reason":"Added exact golden contract for representative external xattr block encoding; verified with focused rch test plus workspace check, clippy, and fmt","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["testing-golden-artifacts"]}
 {"id":"bd-4xbp","title":"Reconcile tracked parity claims with experimental runtime/docs","description":"FEATURE_PARITY.md reports 100% for the tracked V1 matrix, but README/plan/architecture language still mixes that with broader experimental/runtime statements in ways that read as contradictory. Clarify that deterministic unsupported/rejected operations can still be fully covered contract rows, and make the public docs consistently say '100% tracked V1 parity' rather than implying all future/runtime hardening is complete.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-04T01:18:39.971409983Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-04T01:27:25.789900171Z","closed_at":"2026-04-04T01:27:25.789692568Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["docs","parity","porting-to-rust"]}
 {"id":"bd-4xqy5","title":"CONFORMANCE: FUSE mounted file lifecycle ops","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-24T05:27:45.913006830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-24T05:35:00.724912590Z","closed_at":"2026-04-24T05:35:00.722856860Z","close_reason":"Added mounted FUSE file lifecycle matrix covering open, write, flush, read, fsync, release, and reopen.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 > **Canonical source:** This Coverage Summary table in `FEATURE_PARITY.md` is the
 > single source of truth for implemented/total counts. `ParityReport::current()`
@@ -280,7 +280,8 @@ behavior of that operation, not whether the row is unimplemented.
 | FUSE read | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::read` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE readlink | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | `FsOps::readlink` via `OpenFs` |
 | FUSE mount runtime | FrankenFS spec §9 | ✅ | Production runtime lifecycle, signal handling, dispatch coverage, and CI-safe skip behavior are implemented; OQ4 (`bd-h6nz.6.4`) is resolved for V1.x with explicit `flush` (non-durable) vs `fsync`/`fsyncdir` (durability boundary) contract and writeback-cache-disabled policy. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now emits `SCENARIO_RESULT` coverage for ext4 (`ext4_rw_flush`, `ext4_rw_fdatasync`, `ext4_rw_fsync`, `ext4_rw_fsyncdir`, `ext4_rw_seek_data_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `ext4_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `ext4_rw_seek_all_hole`) and btrfs (`btrfs_rw_flush`, `btrfs_rw_fdatasync`, `btrfs_rw_fsync`, `btrfs_rw_fsyncdir`, `btrfs_rw_seek_data_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_hole_fully_allocated`, `btrfs_rw_seek_leading_hole`, `btrfs_rw_seek_all_hole`) through real file flushes, file- and directory-FD sync calls, and mounted-path `SEEK_DATA`/`SEEK_HOLE` probes for punched-hole, fully allocated, leading-hole, and all-hole layouts with EOF `ENXIO` verification; btrfs read-only mounted-path `fsync`, `fdatasync`, and `fsyncdir` now also freeze exact `EROFS` with no data or dirent drift. |
-| FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for the downstream io_uring/RWF behavior slice. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. Downstream behavioral beads still own RWF/io_uring write handling. |
+| FUSE ABI 7.40 protocol surface | FUSE kernel ABI 7.40 / libfuse 3.x | ✅ | Workspace builds the vendored `fuser` crate with `abi-7-40`, and `FrankenFuse::init` now negotiates `FUSE_SPLICE_READ`, `FUSE_SPLICE_WRITE`, `FUSE_SPLICE_MOVE`, and `FUSE_PASSTHROUGH` with stack depth 1 when the kernel advertises support. The vendored `fuser` protocol layer now exposes opcode 52 (`FUSE_STATX`), parses `fuse_statx_in`, replies with Linux statx-compatible `fuse_statx_out`, and preserves `FileAttr::crtime` as `STATX_BTIME`; `FrankenFuse::statx` routes that opcode through the existing `getattr` backend. The vendored ABI surface also exposes `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_SUIDGID`/legacy `FUSE_WRITE_KILL_PRIV` plus Linux `RWF_*` constants for write-intent propagation. Unit coverage freezes ABI constants, STATX request parsing, birth-time reply encoding, and mounted splice pipe-to-file transfer. |
+| FUSE pwritev2/io_uring RWF write intent propagation | `pwritev2(2)`, FUSE `WRITE` request flags | ✅ | `FrankenFuse::write` now decodes synchronous write intent from the FUSE `flags` field (`O_DSYNC`/`O_SYNC`) and routes it through `FsOps::fsync` after the normal MVCC commit, so `RWF_DSYNC` uses a data-sync boundary and `RWF_SYNC` uses a full sync boundary. The implementation deliberately does not treat `fuse_write_in.write_flags` bit 1 as `RWF_DSYNC`, because that bit is already `FUSE_WRITE_LOCKOWNER`; unit coverage freezes that collision and verifies no accidental fsync. `RWF_NOWAIT` uses the non-conflicting RWF bit to attempt inode mutation-lock acquisition without blocking and returns `EAGAIN` when the lock is already held. `crates/ffs-fuse/src/lib.rs` unit tests cover DSync, Sync, collision, and NOWAIT fast-fail behavior, while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` adds a mounted-path `os.pwritev(..., RWF_DSYNC)` probe that soft-skips on hosts without FUSE mount permission. |
 | FUSE inotify rename + dentry notification lifecycle | `fs/notify/fsnotify.c`, `fs/notify/inotify/inotify_user.c`, FUSE notify protocol | ✅ | `FrankenFuse` captures a `fuser::Notifier` during blocking, background, and managed mount setup, then issues `FUSE_NOTIFY_INVAL_ENTRY` for successfully committed `unlink`, `rmdir`, and both old/new directory entries after `rename`. Plain renames deliberately avoid `FUSE_NOTIFY_DELETE`, preserving the kernel/VFS `IN_MOVED_FROM` + `IN_MOVED_TO` cookie-pair contract instead of synthesizing delete/create observations. `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now carries mounted-path inotify probes for 100 same-parent renames and a cross-directory rename; both assert non-zero matching cookies and reject `IN_CREATE`, `IN_DELETE`, `IN_Q_OVERFLOW`, and `IN_IGNORED` during plain rename bursts, with the standard CI soft-skip when the current host denies FUSE mounting. |
 | FUSE ioctl FIEMAP | `include/linux/fiemap.h` | ✅ | `FsOps::fiemap` queries ext4 extent tree via `collect_extents_with_scope` and btrfs extent data items via `btrfs_fiemap_extent_items`. FUSE `ioctl` handler parses `FS_IOC_FIEMAP` (0xC020660B), rejects unsupported request flags with `EBADR`, honors `FIEMAP_FLAG_SYNC` by fsyncing writable mounts before extent lookup, and marshals the fiemap header plus extent array. `crates/ffs-core` now has deterministic btrfs coverage for inline extents (`physical=0`, `FIEMAP_EXTENT_LAST`) and keep-size prealloc extents (`FIEMAP_EXTENT_UNWRITTEN`), while `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` verifies mounted-path FIEMAP reporting for ext4 regular files, ext4 `SYNC` and invalid-request-flag contracts, plus btrfs regular, inline, and keep-size prealloc files. Note: FUSE transport may not deliver this ioctl to userspace on all kernel versions. |
 | FUSE ioctl EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS | `fs/ext4/ioctl.c` | ✅ | `FsOps::get_inode_flags` returns raw ext4 `i_flags` field. FUSE `ioctl` handler dispatches `EXT4_IOC_GETFLAGS` (0x80086601). Harness integration coverage in `crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs` now verifies mounted-path `GETFLAGS` on a writable ext4 file. |

 Rows in the btrfs experimental RW contract can still say `partially supported` or `unsupported` without reducing tracked parity coverage when the expected V1 behavior is a deterministic partial-success or explicit rejection path that is implemented and tested.
